questions.question: Briefly explain why the EVM is described as 'deterministic'. (2-3 marks)
keyPoints: Deterministic means that given the same input and state, the output will always be identical.
This is crucial for consensus, as all nodes must compute the same result independently to agree on the new state of the blockchain.
Non-deterministic operations (e.g., relying on a local random number generator) are not allowed.

questions.question: Describe the EVM's architecture, including its data areas (stack, memory, storage), and explain the role of gas in its operation. (5-10 marks)
keyPoints: The EVM is a stack-based virtual machine. It uses a LIFO stack (max 1024 items) for most computations.
It has volatile Memory (``mem``) for short-term data during execution, which is erased after a transaction.
It has persistent Storage (``sload``/``sstore``) which is part of the world state and holds a contract's permanent data.
Gas is a unit measuring computational effort. Every opcode has a gas cost.
Gas prevents denial-of-service attacks by making infinite loops financially impossible.
Users pay for gas with ETH, which compensates validators for the resources used to execute computations.
